codeReview.summary: The accessibility test file was created with 38 test cases covering 7 pages. However, VAL-A11Y-002 and VAL-A11Y-004 tests have significant gaps that don't actually verify the required assertions.

issues.description: VAL-A11Y-002 tests (Focus indicator exists on login form inputs, navigation links, buttons, etc.) only verify elements CAN receive keyboard focus, not that focus states are VISIBLE. The test pattern `el === document.activeElement` only proves focus was set programmatically, not that a visible focus indicator (ring, outline, background change) exists. The validation contract explicitly requires 'Focused elements have visible focus indicator' with evidence 'screenshot showing focus styles'. Tests should either: (1) use screenshots to capture focus styles, or (2) evaluate CSS computed styles for focus indicators (e.g., outline, box-shadow), or (3) use axe-core or similar to verify focus visibility.

VAL-A11Y-004 tests (Color contrast meets WCAG AA) only verify text has a non-transparent color, not that contrast ratios meet WCAG AA minimums (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text). The test pattern `color !== 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)' && color !== 'transparent' && color !== 'inherit'` passes any visible color including low-contrast combinations like light gray on white. The validation contract requires 'Text contrast ratios meet minimum requirements' with evidence 'contrast check passes'. True WCAG AA verification requires calculating actual luminance ratios using the formula: ((L1 + 0.05) / (L2 + 0.05)) where L is relative luminance.

VAL-A11Y-001 test 'Home page navigation is keyboard accessible' focuses each element individually, but doesn't verify full Tab key navigation through ALL interactive elements in logical order. Consider adding a test that presses Tab repeatedly to verify focus moves through all interactive elements and no element is skipped.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The e2e-worker skill procedure says to use screenshots 'for visual verification when helpful' but doesn't mandate screenshots for accessibility tests. VAL-A11Y-002 explicitly requires screenshot evidence of focus styles per the validation contract, but this wasn't enforced. The skill could be more explicit about when screenshots are required vs optional.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: e2e-worker SKILL.md line 89: 'Use `screenshot` option for visual verification when helpful'. VAL-A11Y-002 contract: 'Evidence: screenshot showing focus styles'. The gap: tests use programmatic focus checks but no screenshots.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No existing accessibility tests in the codebase demonstrate proper focus visibility or WCAG contrast verification patterns. Workers implementing VAL-A11Y-002/004 have no local precedent to follow. Consider adding a convention doc or example test showing expected approach.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No accessibility tests found in e2e/*.spec.ts before this feature. Existing tests use programmatic focus checks but no visual verification.

summary: The e2e-accessibility-comprehensive feature created 38 test cases covering keyboard accessibility, focus states, form labels, and color visibility across 7 pages. However, the tests have blocking gaps: VAL-A11Y-002 tests only verify keyboard focus works, not that focus indicators are visually visible as required by the validation contract. VAL-A11Y-004 tests only verify text color is not transparent, not that WCAG AA contrast ratios are met. These gaps mean the tests would pass even if focus indicators were invisible or text contrast failed accessibility standards.
